encounter.reason: Post-ED follow-up, asthma reclassified severe persistent
encounter.location: Triangle Pediatrics, 4201 Lake Boone Trail, Suite 200, Raleigh, NC 27607

conditions.name: asthma, severe persistent
conditions.note: Previously classified as moderate persistent. Reclassified to severe persistent based on ED-level exacerbation despite adherence to Advair HFA 45/21 BID and montelukast, rescue inhaler use 3–4 times/week at baseline, frequent nighttime symptoms, and multiple school absences.

medications.name: fluticasone/salmeterol (Advair HFA 115/21) 1 puff inhaled twice daily
medications.sig: Inhale 1 puff by mouth twice daily using spacer with mask

medications.name: fluticasone/salmeterol (Advair HFA 45/21) 1 puff inhaled twice daily
medications.reason: severe persistent asthma — dose inadequate, stepped up to 115/21

medications.name: montelukast 4 mg chewable tablet
medications.sig: Take 1 chewable tablet by mouth once daily at bedtime

medications.name: albuterol (ProAir HFA 90 mcg) 2 puffs inhaled
medications.sig: Inhale 2 puffs by mouth every 4–6 hours as needed for wheezing or shortness of breath, using spacer

medications.name: prednisolone 18 mg oral
medications.sig: Take 18 mg by mouth once daily; complete remaining 2 days of 5-day course

document_references.description: Acute visit progress note for post-ED follow-up. Documents subjective improvement since ED discharge for severe asthma exacerbation, vitals with SpO2 97% on room air, exam showing scattered end-expiratory wheezes without distress, reclassification of asthma from moderate persistent to severe persistent, step-up of ICS/LABA therapy to Advair HFA 115/21, continuation of prednisolone burst, re-referral to pediatric allergy for biologic therapy consideration, and follow-up plan.

Updated asthma action plan reflecting the change from Advair HFA 45/21 to Advair HFA 115/21 as the daily controller medication, with green/yellow/red zone instructions and emergency guidance for severe persistent asthma classification.
Updated 504 plan documentation and school nurse notification reflecting the change in asthma classification to severe persistent and the new controller medication (Advair HFA 115/21).
